Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: * Input: A description of a loan application process divided into two parts: Normative Process Behavior (Strict Rules) and Desiderata (Recommended Best Practices). There is also an Event Log with three cases (Case 1, Case 2, Case 3). * Task: 1. Identify normative violations (strict rule breaches) with explanations. 2. Identify desiderata failures (best practice breaches) with clarification on severity. 3. Suggest interpretations/justifications for observed behaviors where possible. * Output Format: Structured analysis based on the tasks above.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Normative Rules (Strict): * Sequence: Application Received → Credit Check → Risk Assessment → Loan Decision → Inform Applicant → [If Approved: Finalize Documentation] → Close Case. * Ordering: Credit Check < Risk Assessment. Risk Assessment < Loan Decision. Loan Decision < Inform Applicant. Approved → Finalize Documentation < Close Case. Rejected → NO Finalize Documentation before Close Case. * Forbidden: Skipping steps, reversing order, Finalize Documentation after Rejection.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 3. Analyze the Desiderata (Recommended): * Credit Check within 24 hours of Application Received. * Inform Applicant within 48 hours of Loan Decision. * Follow-up Call before Close Case (encouraged, not required).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Analyze the Event Log (Case by Case): * **Case 1:** * Sequence: App (09:00) → Credit (09:30) → Risk (10:00) → Decision (11:00, Approved) → Inform (13:00) → Doc (13:30) → Close (14:00). * Normative Check: Credit before Risk? Yes. Risk before Decision? Yes. Decision before Inform? Yes. Approved → Doc before Close? Yes. Any skipped steps? No. * Desiderata Check: Credit Check time: 30 mins (< 24h). OK. Inform time: 2 hours (< 48h). OK. Follow-up Call: Not mentioned. * Verdict: Compliant.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Case 2:** * Sequence: App (08:00) → Risk (08:20) → Credit (09:00) → Decision (09:30, Rejected) → Doc (10:00) → Close (10:30). * Normative Check: Credit before Risk? NO. Risk (08:20) before Credit (09:00). Risk before Decision? Yes. Decision before Inform? NO. Inform Applicant is missing entirely. Rejected → Doc before Close? NO. Documentation was finalized despite rejection. * Desiderata Check: Credit Check time: 1 hour (< 24h). OK. Inform time: N/A. * Verdict: Multiple Normative Violations.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Case 3:** * Sequence: App (09:00) → Credit (10:00 next day) → Risk (11:00) → Decision (12:00, Approved) → Inform (16:00 2 days later) → Doc (17:00) → Close (18:00). * Normative Check: Credit before Risk? Yes. Risk before Decision? Yes. Decision before Inform? Yes. Approved → Doc before Close? Yes. Any skipped steps? No. * Desiderata Check: Credit Check time: ~50 hours (> 24h). Violation. Inform time: ~51 hours (> 48h). Violation. Follow-up Call: Not present. Violation (of recommendation). * Verdict: Normatively Compliant, Desiderata Violations.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 5. Drafting the Response: * Refining Justifications: * Case 2: System glitch? Human error? Rushed process? * Case 3: Resource constraints? Delays in external credit bureaus? Staffing issues?

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 6. Review against Constraints: * Did I clearly distinguish between Normative (Strict) and Desiderata (Recommended)? Yes. * Did I explain why for each violation? Yes. * Did I address the Inform Applicant missing step in Case 2? Yes, thats a critical normative gap. * Did I address the Finalize Documentation after Rejection in Case 2? Yes.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 7. Final Polish: Ensure tone is professional and analytical. (Self-Correction: Make sure to explicitly mention that Case 1 is fully compliant to set a baseline). *Wait, looking closer at Case 2:* The log says Inform Applicant is missing. The normative rule says Following the loan decision, the applicant must be informed of the outcome. So missing this is a violation. *Wait, looking closer at Case 3:* The log explicitly notes the desideratum violations in parentheses. I need to confirm those are the only ones. Yes.
